name: White Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt chocolate over hot (not boiling) water. Cool slightly.
2. Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Add cooled chocolate. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. Sift dry ingredients together and add alternately with buttermilk. Beat only enough to blend.
4. Fold in beaten egg whites, vanilla, pecans, and coconut. Pour into a 9x13 inch pan or two 8-inch round pans.
5. Bake at 350°F for 40-45 minutes or until done.
White Chocolate Icing:
1. In a medium saucepan, combine melted chocolate and flour (all-purpose). Blend in milk, cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thick. Cool completely.
2. In a large mixing bowl, cream butter, sugar, and vanilla. Beat until light and fluffy. Gradually add the completely cooled chocolate mixture. Beat to blend well; over-beating will make it too soupy.
